Try with:

How Photosynthesis Works — Biology Basics

1,842 words · English · TXT SRT VTT PDF DOCX

00:00Welcome back — today we’re looking at how photosynthesis actually works.
00:14Plants take in carbon dioxide through tiny pores called stomata.
00:31Inside the chloroplast, light energy splits water molecules apart.
00:52That releases the oxygen we breathe — a by-product of the process.
01:10The energy gets stored as glucose, which fuels the whole plant.
01:27So the equation is: carbon dioxide plus water, powered by light…

The Fast, Free YouTube Transcript Generator

Paste any YouTube link and get a clean, timestamped transcript in seconds — free, unlimited, and with no signup required.

Fast Processing

Transcripts are fetched and formatted in seconds, so you can get straight to reading, quoting, or repurposing the content.

125+ Languages

Works with videos in any language that has captions, with automatic language detection for the best available transcript.

Privacy First

No signup, no stored URLs, no saved transcripts. Every request is processed independently and nothing is retained.

Multiple Formats

Copy the timestamped text or download as TXT, SRT, VTT, PDF, or Word files — ready for editors, players, and documents.

YouTube to Transcript in Three Steps

Turning a YouTube video into text takes seconds — no software, no Chrome extension, and no account. Here’s the whole process.

1

Paste the YouTube link

Copy any YouTube URL — from the address bar, or the Share button in the mobile app — and paste it into the box above. Full links, youtu.be short links, and yt transcript lookups all work.

2

Generate the transcript

Press the button and TranscriptFlow pulls the video’s captions and builds a clean, timestamped transcript in a few seconds — even for videos with only auto-generated captions.

3

Copy, translate, or download

Copy the YouTube transcript to your clipboard, translate it into any language the video supports, or download it as TXT, SRT, VTT, PDF, or Word — named after the video.

Need something specific? Translate a transcript, download subtitles as SRT/VTT, grab a whole playlist transcript, or read the step-by-step guide.

What People Use Transcripts For

Learning & Research

Turn lectures, tutorials, and interviews into searchable notes you can skim, quote, and cite.

Content Repurposing

Convert videos into blog posts, social snippets, show notes, and newsletters in minutes.

SEO for Video

Publish transcript text alongside your videos so search engines can index every word you say.

Accessibility

Give deaf and hard-of-hearing audiences, and anyone in a sound-off environment, full access to your content.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is TranscriptFlow really free to use?

Yes. TranscriptFlow is free with no hidden costs, subscriptions, or usage caps. You can generate transcripts without creating an account or entering payment details.

How accurate are the generated transcripts?

Transcripts are based on the captions available for the video, including YouTube’s automatic captions. Accuracy is excellent for clear speech and may vary with audio quality, accents, and background noise.

What languages are supported?

Over 125 languages, including English, Spanish, French, German, Chinese, Japanese, Arabic, and Hindi. The video’s language is detected automatically.

How fast is the transcript generation?

Most transcripts are ready in a few seconds. Very long videos can take slightly longer while the full transcript is formatted.

Do you store my video URLs or transcripts?

No. TranscriptFlow doesn’t store video URLs, generated transcripts, or any personal information. Each request is processed independently without data retention.

Which videos can be transcribed?

Any public YouTube video that has captions or subtitles available. Private videos, or videos whose owners disabled captions, can’t be transcribed.

How do I copy a YouTube transcript?

Paste the video link, generate the transcript, then click Copy Text to copy the whole thing to your clipboard. You can also download it as TXT, SRT, VTT, PDF, or Word instead of copying.

How do I get a YouTube transcript without an extension?

TranscriptFlow runs in your browser — no Chrome extension or install needed. Paste the YouTube URL on the site and the transcript appears in seconds, on any device.

Does this work on a phone?

Yes. It works in any mobile browser on Android or iPhone. Copy the video’s Share link in the YouTube app, paste it here, and get the transcript — no app to install.

Ready to Get Started?

Paste a link, get the words. It really is that simple.

No signup required • Completely free • Instant results

How It Works

Transform any YouTube video into text in just three simple steps. No registration required, completely free to use.

1

Paste YouTube URL

Simply copy and paste any YouTube video URL into our input field. We support all YouTube video formats including shorts.

2

Caption Processing

Our engine locates the best available captions for the video, including auto-generated ones, and cleans them into a readable transcript.

3

Download & Use

Get your formatted transcript instantly. Copy to clipboard, download as TXT, SRT, or VTT files for your projects.

Ready to Try It?

Start generating transcripts in seconds. No account needed.

From education to business, content creation to research - TranscriptFlow serves professionals across industries with accurate, fast transcription.

Ready to Transform Your Workflow?

Whether you're a student, professional, or content creator, TranscriptFlow has the tools you need to convert video content into actionable text.

About TranscriptFlow

TranscriptFlow is an independently built and run tool with one job: get you the words of any YouTube video — fast, clean, and in the format you actually need. It fetches the captions that already exist for a video (including YouTube's auto-generated ones), formats them with timestamps, and hands them to you as text, subtitles, documents, or translations.

No accounts, no stored data. We don't keep your video URLs or transcripts — every request is processed and forgotten. Your "Recent" list lives only in your own browser. That's not a marketing line; it's how the system is built.

How it stays free. Single-video transcripts are free without limits, supported by ads and by TranscriptFlow Pro — bulk playlist exports for people who need transcripts at scale. No venture capital, no growth hacks, no dark patterns: if the tool is useful, it funds itself.
